title: 10 best herbal decoctions for psoriasis
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for psoriasis are a traditional remedy that has been used to alleviate symptoms of the chronic skin condition. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water, releasing their active compounds and anti-inflammatory properties. Turmeric, chamomile, and oatmeal decoctions have shown promise in reducing redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. By using these herbal decoctions, individuals with psoriasis can experience relief from discomfort and embarrassment, allowing them to live more confidently and comfortably.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe decoctions helps with psoriasis because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and redness associated with the condition. The decoctions also have antioxidant properties, which help to repair damaged skin cells and promote collagen production, leading to improved skin elasticity and flexibility. Additionally, aloe vera has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of psoriasis. By using herbal aloe decoctions topically, individuals with psoriasis may experience relief from itching, scaling, and redness, promoting healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal aloe decoctions have anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a hallmark symptom of psoriasis. This reduces redness, swelling, and pain associated with the condition.

Antioxidant property: Aloe vera's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms and promote chronic inflammation.

Immunomodulatory property: Herbal aloe decoctions contain immunomodulatory compounds that help regulate the immune system's response to psoriasis, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promoting a balanced immune response.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in aloin, a compound that helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, making it effective in treating psoriasis symptoms.
Leaves: Containing high amounts of vitamin E and other antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin cell growth.
remedy_recipe: Wash and chop fresh aloe vera leaves into small pieces weighing about one hundred fifty grams.
Combine the chopped aloe vera with two liters of distilled water in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for twenty-five minutes or until the liquid reduces to one liter.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator at a temperature of three degrees celsius for up to five days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with psoriasis because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can soothe and calm irritated skin. The decoction's rich concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and carotenoids works to reduce redness and swelling associated with psoriatic lesions, while its antifungal and antibacterial properties combat underlying infections that may exacerbate the condition. Additionally, pot marigold decoctions have been shown to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation in skin tissue, making them a valuable natural remedy for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: The flavonoids and terpenes present in calendula decoctions can reduce inflammation, which is a primary symptom of psoriasis.
Antioxidant activity: Calendula's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from oxidative stress, damage caused by free radicals, and DNA mutations that contribute to psoriasis development.

Wound-healing and antimicrobial effects: The decoction's ability to promote wound healing and inhibit microbial growth may aid in reducing symptoms of psoriasis, such as itching, scaling, and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and promoting healing in psoriasis patients.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the pot marigold plant have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, and are believed to help reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried calendula flowers for each day's treatment.
Rinse the dried flowers with boiling water to rehydrate them for 10-15 minutes.
Combine the rehydrated flowers with 500ml of distilled water in a glass jar for steeping.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 30-45 minutes then strain and discard solids.
Use the resulting clear decoction as a topical treatment applying 20-25ml to affected areas.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric decoctions helps with psoriasis because it contains curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. This natural remedy has been shown to improve skin health by reducing symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of turmeric also help to reduce joint pain and stiffness, common comorbidities with psoriasis. By incorporating turmeric decoctions into a daily routine, individuals with psoriasis may experience improved overall skin health and reduced symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Turmeric contains powerful anti-inflammatory compounds like curcumin, which reduces inflammation and prevents further damage to skin tissues, helping to alleviate symptoms of psoriasis.

Antioxidant activity: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and environmental factors that can trigger or exacerbate psoriasis, promoting healthy skin cell function and reducing signs of the condition.
Immune system modulation: Turmeric has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing overactive immune responses that contribute to psoriasis symptoms, such as redness, scaling, and itching.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of curcumin, a powerful anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with psoriasis.
Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like curcuminoids, which have been shown to possess potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric roots and wash them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the washed turmeric roots in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours.
Grind 10g of dried turmeric powder into fine particles using a spice grinder.
Steep 5g of ground turmeric powder in 500ml of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, allowing the liquid to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its unique composition of antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common triggers of the condition. The decoction also possesses anti-proliferative properties, which help to slow down the rapid skin cell growth associated with psoriasis. Additionally, the decoction's moisturizing and soothing effects can help to calm redness and itchiness, providing relief from symptoms and promoting a healthy, clear complexion.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidants and polyphenols may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms.

Immunomodulatory properties: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to modulate the immune system by reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines and increasing anti-inflammatory cytokines, which could potentially help alleviate psoriasis symptoms.
Antiproliferative effects: Ginkgo biloba's compounds may inhibit the growth of skin cells that contribute to psoriasis lesions, helping to reduce their size and severity.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree are used to make decoctions that help reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis, while also promoting skin healing.
Bark: The bark of the maidenhair tree contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing redness and swelling caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and two cups of pure water.
Bring the mixture to a boil in a medium-sized saucepan over high heat for three minutes.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Siberian ginseng decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its ability to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are common underlying factors in the disease. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe itchy and irritated skin, while its antioxidant compounds can combat free radicals that contribute to cell damage. Additionally, Siberian ginseng has been shown to modulate the immune system, reducing excessive inflammation and promoting a balanced response. By addressing these underlying causes, herbal Siberian ginseng decoctions may provide relief from psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Siberian Ginseng decoctions contain antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to the development of psoriasis symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with psoriasis, making it a potential natural remedy for soothing skin irritations.

Immune system modulation: Siberian Ginseng has been shown to regulate immune cell activity, which can help mitigate the overactive immune response that contributes to psoriasis development and exacerbation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and improve skin health.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes have anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing and calming the skin affected by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried eleutherococcus senticosus root, rinsed clean from any debris.
Measure 10 cups of pure water and pour it over the herbs in a large pot, ensuring proper coverage.
Bring the mixture to a boil on high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 1 hour.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool, then refrigerate it at 39°f to 45°f for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The decoction has been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, which is a hallmark symptom of psoriasis. Additionally, St John's wort has been found to modulate the immune system, reducing the activation of T-cells that contribute to the development of psoriatic lesions. By calming the inflammatory response and regulating the immune system, St John's wort decoctions can help alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and scaling associated with psoriasis.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: St John's Wort has been shown to have potent antioxidant effects, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, common underlying factors in psoriasis.

Anti-inflammatory activity: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with psoriasis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Immunomodulatory effects: St John's Wort has been found to modulate immune responses, which can help regulate an overactive immune system that contributes to psoriasis symptoms such as plaque formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St. John's Wort contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation and improve skin health.

Barks: The barks of St. John's Wort contain hyperforin, a compound known for its ability to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal decoctions for psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves according to traditional guidelines.
Measure out two teaspoons of dried hypericum perforatum per cup of cold water.
Combine the dried herbs and water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup or pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container, discard solids and let cool to room temperature before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal decoctions helps with psoriasis because its active compounds, berberine and isoquinoline alkaloids, have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the skin, which can alleviate symptoms of psoriasis such as redness, swelling, and itching. Additionally, goldenseal's antibacterial and antifungal properties may help to combat infections that can exacerbate psoriasis, promoting a healthier skin environment and reducing the occurrence of flares.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infections that can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms.

Benzylisoquinoline Alkaloids (BIAs): Possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and regulate the immune response to reduce psoriasis symptoms.

Berberastin: Exhibits anti-proliferative and pro-apoptotic properties, which can help reduce the rapid growth of skin cells that is characteristic of psoriasis and induce programmed cell death (apoptosis) in affected cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated skin.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried goldenseal root powder with 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Mix 10-15 grams of hydrastis canadensis root powder with 2 cups of cold distilled water.
Simmer the mixture in a double boiler at 100°f to 110°f for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled and filtered decoction in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm decoctions helps with psoriasis because its mucilaginous properties soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and redness. The decoction's emollient effects also help to moisturize and soften dry, scaly skin patches, promoting a healthier skin barrier. Additionally, slippery elm contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce the production of cytokines, which contribute to psoriasis flares. By providing gentle, non-irritating relief, slippery elm decoctions can be a valuable complementary therapy for managing psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory activity: Slippery elm's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with psoriatic lesions.
Sotheing and protective mucilages: The mucilage content in slippery elm helps soothe and protect the skin from further irritation, reducing itching and discomfort caused by psoriasis.
Immune system modulation: Slippery elm's immunomodulatory effects can help regulate an overactive immune response, which is believed to contribute to the development of psoriasis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are a rich source of mucilages, which help to soothe and moisturize the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with psoriasis.
Barks: The barks contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that aid in reducing redness, swelling, and itching caused by psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra bark or dry it for future use.
Cut and chop the bark into small pieces.
Combine the chopped bark with water in a pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain and store the decoction in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the condition. The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root also helps to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a healthy environment for healing. Additionally, licorice decoctions have been shown to improve skin elasticity, firmness, and hydration, which can help to reduce psoriasis symptoms such as dryness and scaling. As a result, using herbal licorice decoctions may provide relief from psoriasis-related discomfort and promote overall skin health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root has been shown to reduce inflammation, which is a common underlying factor in psoriasis, by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.
Immunomodulatory property: Licorice root has immunosuppressive effects, which can help modulate an overactive immune response that can contribute to psoriasis symptoms such as redness and irritation.

Antioxidant property: Licorice root is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate psoriasis symptoms like itching, scaling, and thickened skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making it effective in reducing redness and irritation associated with psoriasis.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing, making them beneficial for treating psoriasis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots in quantities of 10-15 grams for each day's dose.
Rinse the dried roots thoroughly with distilled water to remove any impurities.
Chop the roots into small pieces and transfer them to a heat-resistant container.
Combine one part chopped root with eight parts boiling water and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container and discard solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with psoriasis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpene lactones, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the skin, thus alleviating symptoms like redness, itching, and scaling. Additionally, dandelion decoctions may help modulate the immune response, which is often disrupted in psoriasis patients. By soothing and calming the skin, these natural extracts can provide relief from this chronic condition, promoting a clearer, healthier complexion.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation and redness associated with psoriasis, promoting healing and skin calmness.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in dandelion decoction neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, reducing oxidative stress and helping to prevent further psoriasis flares.

Laxative properties: Dandelion's natural laxative effects help remove toxins and excess waste from the body, which can contribute to psoriasis symptoms by reducing inflammation and improving overall gut health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of psoriasis, such as redness and scaling.

Seeds: The seeds of the dandelion plant contain a compound called taraxasterol, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe skin irritation associated with psoriasis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality taraxacum officinale roots and rhizomes of 2-3 year-old plants.
Cleanly wash the roots and rhizomes with cold running water to remove any debris or dirt.
Chop the cleaned roots and rhizomes into small pieces, then macerate them in a ratio of 1 part herb to 4 parts water for 30 minutes.
Bring the macerated mixture to a boil in a large pot, then reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat psoriasis with herbal decoctions is by brewing a strong tea from a combination of turmeric, ginger, and licorice root. This blend has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce redness and itching associated with the condition. Boil 1-2 teaspoons of dried herbs in 1 cup of water for 10-15 minutes, then strain and drink 2-3 times a day to alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to psoriasis that are treated with herbal decoctions are eczema, dermatitis, and vitiligo. These conditions, characterized by skin inflammation and discoloration, can be alleviated with the use of herbal remedies like neem, turmeric, and aloe vera. Decoctions made from these herbs have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe itchy skin, reduce redness, and promote healing. Additionally, decoctions may also be used to treat other skin conditions such as acne, rosacea, and minor wounds.
